headline: Sam Altman's project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
angle: The absurdity of needing an iris-scanning orb to prove you're human on a dating app where people already lie about everything else.
scene: A man sitting at a laptop on a dating app, swiping through profiles. Next to him is an ominous black spherical device with a glowing iris scanner (the Orb). He looks exhausted. A notification pops up: 'Verify your humanity before you can verify your intentions.'
setup: Finally, a dating app that checks if you're actually human.
punchline: Too bad it can't verify if you're being honest about your job.
instagram_caption: Sam Altman's iris scanner arrives just in time to solve dating's real problem: authenticity.
image_prompt: Single-panel cartoon, clean line art, thick black outlines, muted blue-gray pastel colors, white background, New Yorker magazine aesthetic, flat colors, no shading, no gradients, no signature. A tired man sitting at a computer desk with a laptop open showing a dating app interface. Next to the laptop is a large black spherical device with a glowing ring and iris scanner (the Orb). The man's expression is resigned. The glow from both the laptop and the Orb illuminates his face in the dim room. A notification bubble visible on the laptop screen.
